events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves NM/MC cable routing & protection on a residential job, with unsupported runs observed. Work is in-progress. Need to assess hazards, severity, controls.

Unsupported NM/MC cable runs violate NEC 334.30 (NM cable must be supported every 4.5 ft and within 12 in of boxes) and 330.30 (MC cable similar support requirements). NEC 300.4 covers protection from physical damage. Unsupported runs can lead to sagging, physical damage, strain on connections, and potential fire/shock hazards.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, potentially unsafe)
Risk: medium (unsupported runs during in-progress work could lead to physical damage but not immediately critical)
Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective
Percent complete: maybe 40% (in-progress, rough-in stage)
Confidence: 0.85

Findings:
- NM/MC cable runs not supported per NEC 334.30/330.30 (supports required every 4.5 ft, within 12 in of boxes)
- Cables subject to sagging, physical damage, and strain at terminations
- Potential violation of NEC 300.4 protection requirements if run through/along areas subject to damage

Actions:
- Install approved supports (staples, straps, hangers) at required intervals and within 12 in of boxes
- Secure cables to prevent sagging and movement
- Verify cables are protected from physical damage per 300.4 where required
- Re-inspect after corrections

References: NEC 300.4, 334.30, 330.30
